Aditya Birla Sun Life Insurance Akshaya Plan – Frequently Asked Questions
ABSLI Akshaya Plan – Summary
1. ABSLI Akshaya Plan is a non-linked, participating individual savings life insurance plan which offers comprehensive life insurance cover for the entire duration of the policy term. The plan offers 2 benefit options – Long Term Income and Whole Life Income (till age 100 or age 85). Being a participating product, this plan provides non-guaranteed Cash bonus from the end of 1st policy year or as per the Cash Bonus payout frequency chosen. The customer has the option to receive the Cash Bonus in annual, semi-annual, quarterly or monthly frequency and the same shall be payable at the end of the year, half-year, quarter or month, as the case may be. The customer also has the flexibility to defer the declared Cash Bonus (if declared) and accrue them instead in the form of Accumulated Cash Bonus.
What is ABSLI Akshaya Plan?
2. What are the key features of ABSLI Akshaya Plan?
Key features of ABSLI Akshaya Plan are:
✓ Flexible Bonus Payouts: Complete flexibility to receive Cash Bonus (if

The following details are about Akshaya Plan(AP).

Key features of ABSLI Akshaya Plan are:
✓ Flexible Bonus Payouts: Complete flexibility to receive Cash Bonus (if declared) - for immediate liquidity or to accumulate the Cash Bonus (if declared) till policy maturity - for long term wealth creation
✓ Liquidity: Option to get instant liquidity through Cash Bonus (if declared) from the end of the first month/year
✓ Choice of 2 Benefit Options: Flexibility to choose amongst two Benefit Options i.e. Long Term Income and Whole Life Income (Till Age 100 or Till Age 85) to cater to the customers family’s needs across all life stages
✓ Financial Security for ones loved ones: Comprehensive life cover available for up to 100 years
✓ Flexibility: PPT's starting as low as 6 years, with option to get Cash Bonus (if declared) till the age of 100 years
✓ Enhanced Benefits: Higher ticket sizes are rewarded with additional Cash Bonus (multiple premium bands)
✓ Loyalty Discount: Existing customers of ABSLI are rewarded through first year premium discount
✓ Customizable Benefits: Option to enhance the insurance cover through riders, which are available at a nominal

Bonus (multiple premium bands)

extra cost.
3. What is the age criteria for this Plan?
Anyone meeting the following criteria is eligible for ABSLI Akshaya Plan:
- Age at entry: 30 days to 55 years (age last birthday) Please Note:- The maximum entry age for premium band Rs 24,000 - 49,999 is restricted to 45 years.
4. What is the maximum maturity age applicable for the policy? The maximum maturity age for Long Term Income is 84 years and for Whole Life Income it is 85 or 100 years (based on Whole Life Income variant chosen.)

20. What are the rules applicable for a person to get existing customer discount?
Discount will be applied only if Proposer has ABSLI policy as policy owner or the person had any ABSLI policy previously as policy owner which was not cancelled as free look. Benefits
21. What is the Bonus Philosophy under this plan? Cash Bonus
▪ Non-guaranteed Cash Bonus shall be payable from the end of 1st policy year or as per the Cash Bonus payout frequency chosen, till the earlier of death of the Life Insured if such death results in termination of the Policy, Surrender or Maturity Date.

The following details are about Akshaya Plan(AP).
Bonus shall be payable from the end of 1st policy year or as per the Cash Bonus payout frequency chosen, till the earlier of death of the Life Insured if such death results in termination of the Policy, Surrender or Maturity Date.
▪ The bonus shall be declared in the form of a Cash Bonus rate every year.
▪ Cash Bonus rate is expressed as per 1000 Sum Assured and once declared shall be payable on the next policy anniversary.
▪ The effective date for the annual bonus rates declared shall be 1st July of every year.
Interim Bonus
The Company may pay an Interim Bonus (if declared), in case of death of the Life Insured, surrender, survival or maturity happens before the latest declared bonus rates would have come into effect.
Terminal Bonus
Terminal bonus (if declared) may be payable on earlier of death of the Life Insured or surrender or maturity.
22. Does the Policyholder have to choose the Cash Bonus payout option at the time of policy issuance?
No, the default option at policy inception is Cash Bonus. However, after policy inception, the Policyholder has the flexibility to defer the declared Cash Bonus (if declared) and accrue them instead in the form of Accumulated Cash Bonus.

The following details are about Akshaya Plan(AP).
 However, after policy inception, the Policyholder has the flexibility to defer the declared Cash Bonus (if declared) and accrue them instead in the form of Accumulated Cash Bonus.
23. What is the Survival Benefit available in the plan?
Provided the policy is in-force and all due premiums under the policy have been paid, the Policyholder will receive the Cash Bonus (if declared), starting from the first policy year or as the Cash Bonus Payout Frequency chosen until Policy Maturity.
24. Can I accumulate the Bonus, instead of taking Cash Bonus?
Yes, the Policyholder shall have the flexibility to defer the declared Cash Bonus (if declared) and accrue them instead in the form of Accumulated Cash Bonus
25. What is the interest that I will earn on the Accumulated Cash Bonus?
The Cash Bonus shall be accumulated monthly at reverse repo rate published by Reserve Bank of India (RBI) on its website. This rate will be reviewed at the beginning of every quarter and will be aligned with the latest reverse-repo rate published on RBI’s website. The current reverse repo rate is 3.35%.

The following details are about Akshaya Plan(AP).
 This rate will be reviewed at the beginning of every quarter and will be aligned with the latest reverse-repo rate published on RBI’s website. The current reverse repo rate is 3.35%.
26. Can I withdraw from the Accumulated Cash Bonus?
Yes, the Policyholder can withdraw the Accumulated Cash Bonus partly or fully at any point during the Policy Term. The withdrawal can only be made if the lump sum amount payable is at least Rs. 1,000.
27. Are there any charges or tax deductions on each withdrawal made from Accumulated Cash Bonus? No, there is no charge/cost or tax deduction associated with each withdrawal amount.
28. What details are required to place a withdrawal request from Accumulated Cash Bonus? Is there any specific documentation needed? The Policyholder will need to submit his/her request at the nearest Branch. The Policyholder will also in due course of time be able to use his/her customer portal login credentials to place a request for withdrawal.

29. Can I switch between Cash Bonus and Accumulated Cash Bonus?
Yes, the Policyholder can choose to opt in or opt out of Accumulated Cash Bonus feature at any point during the Policy Term by giving a written notice to ABSLI at least 30 days before the policy anniversary and the same shall be applicable from the next policy anniversary.
30. What is the Death Benefit during the policy term under this plan?
In the event of death of the Life Insured during the Policy Term, provided that the Policy is in-force and not under Lapsed or Reduced Paid-Up mode, We will pay the following to the nominee(s)/beneficiary(ies):
a. Sum Assured on Death; and,
b. Accumulated Cash Bonus (if declared); and,
c. Terminal Bonus (if declared)
On payment of Death Benefit, the policy will terminate and no future benefits will be payable.
Sum Assured on Death shall be defined as higher of the following:
• Sum Assured
• 11 times the Annualized Premium
• 105% of Total Premiums paid till the date of death
31.

The following details are about Akshaya Plan(AP).

Sum Assured on Death shall be defined as higher of the following:
• Sum Assured
• 11 times the Annualized Premium
• 105% of Total Premiums paid till the date of death
31. Can the Death benefit be taken in instalments?
No, this plan does not offer Staggered Death Benefit Option to the nominee.
32. What is the Maturity Benefit under this Plan?
On survival of the Life Insured till the end of the Policy Term provided the Policy is in-force and all due Installment Premiums under the Policy have been paid, the following will be payable:
a. Sum Assured; and,
b. Accumulated Cash Bonus (if declared); and
c. Terminal Bonus (if declared)
33. When does the Policy acquire a Surrender Value? Policy acquires a Surrender Value once premiums for at least two full policy years have been paid.
34. What is the Surrender Benefit under this plan?
The surrender value payable to policyholder will be higher of
• Guaranteed Surrender Value or
• Special Surrender Value
Where,
Guaranteed Surrender Value (GSV) is defined as: (Guaranteed Surrender Value Factor multiplied by Total Premiums Paid) Minus (Cash Bonus (if declared) already paid).

The following details are about Akshaya Plan(AP).
or
• Special Surrender Value
Where,
Guaranteed Surrender Value (GSV) is defined as: (Guaranteed Surrender Value Factor multiplied by Total Premiums Paid) Minus (Cash Bonus (if declared) already paid).
Special Surrender Value (SSV) is determined by the Company from time to time basis changing economic scenario. The Company may revise the SSV factors based on the then prevailing market conditions. Any change in the methodology/formula for calculating the SSV factors shall be subject to IRDAI approval.
The Accumulated Cash Bonus (if declared) shall be paid over and above the Surrender Benefit.
35. What are the terms and conditions applicable for revival of this policy?
The Policyholder can revive the policy within a revival period of five years from the due date of first unpaid premium, subject to following conditions:
• Paying all outstanding premiums together with interest and/or late fees as declared by the Company from time to time.

Once the policy has been revived, on the effective date of revival, all benefits will be restored to their full value. The monthly interest rate charged on unpaid premiums will be declared by ABSLI on June 1st of each calendar year. The current applicable compound interest rate, as declared on June 1st, 2022, is 1% per month (compounded). Any change in basis of determination of interest rate for revival can be done only after prior approval of IRDAI. If a lapsed policy is not revived within five years, the policy shall be terminated, and no value is payable to the Policyholder.
36. What happens if a policy is not revived within the revival period?
In case the policy is not revived within the revival period, the policy cannot be revived thereafter and the Lapsed Policy shall stand

The following details are about Akshaya Plan(AP).

In case the policy is not revived within the revival period, the policy cannot be revived thereafter and the Lapsed Policy shall stand terminated with no benefits payable thereafter. However, a Reduced Paid Up policy shall continue under the Reduced Paid-up mode till the end of the policy term.
37. What happens in case of death of the Life Insured by suicide?
If the Life Insured dies by suicide within 12 months of the effective date of commencement of risk or the date of revival of policy, the policy shall terminate immediately. In such cases, the Company shall pay higher of Surrender Value or Sum of (Total Premiums Paid, underwriting extra premiums paid, loadings for modal premiums paid) in case the policy has acquired a Surrender Value; or Total premiums Paid plus underwriting extra premiums paid plus loadings for modal premiums paid, in case the policy has not acquired a Surrender Value.
38. Is there any rider benefit available in this plan?
Yes, Policyholder can enhance insurance coverage during the policy term by adding one or more of the following riders for a nominal extra cost.

The following details are about Akshaya Plan(AP).

Yes, Policyholder can enhance insurance coverage during the policy term by adding one or more of the following riders for a nominal extra cost. ABSLI Critical Illness Rider (UIN: 109B019V03) ABSLI Surgical Care Rider (UIN: 109B015V03) ABSLI Hospital Care Rider (UIN: 109B016V03) ABSLI Waiver of Premium Rider (UIN: 109B017V03)
ABSLI Accidental Death Benefit Rider Plus (UIN: 109B023V02)
WOP rider will not be available when the Life Insured and Proposer are different and Life Insured is a major (above 18 years of age). Please refer to detailed brochures on riders or visit our website for further details.
39. Is loan facility available under this plan?
Yes, policy loan is allowed once the policy acquires a surrender value. The minimum loan amount is Rs.5,000 and the maximum is up to 80% of surrender value less any outstanding policy loan plus all accrued but unpaid loan interest as on that date. On exercising this option, the policy shall automatically get assigned to ABSLI to the extent of the outstanding policy loan balance.
Where the policy is in-force (premium paying) or fully paid up and the outstanding policy loan balance

The following details are about Akshaya Plan(AP).

Where the policy is in-force (premium paying) or fully paid up and the outstanding policy loan balance at any time is equal to or exceeds the then prevailing Surrender Value, ABSLI shall send a notice, 90 days in advance, to the Policyholder to repay the loan amount along with the interest.
For other than in-force and fully paid up policies, on the date the outstanding policy loan balance exceeds the Surrender Value, ABSLI shall send a notice, 90 days in advance, to the Policyholder to repay the loan amount along with the interest. In case the Policyholder fails to respond to the notice, the policy will be terminated.
Any payment of a proceed against Death, Survival Benefit or Maturity Benefit provisions or as a Surrender Value shall be reduced by any outstanding policy loan balance and interest thereon at that time and the residual value shall be payable. We shall be issuing the loan re-payment schedule at the time the Policyholder opts for the loan against policy. At the beginning of a policy year, the policy shall be assigned the latest Loan Interest Rate declared by company.

The following details are about Akshaya Plan(AP).
 At the beginning of a policy year, the policy shall be assigned the latest Loan Interest Rate declared by company. We shall declare the Loan Interest Rate applicable to all policies under this product on June 1st of every calendar year which shall be assigned to policies on their next policy anniversary and is equal to the base rate of the State bank of India plus 100 basis points. The interest rate applicable as on June 1st, 2022 is 8.55%p.a. (compounded).

40. Can Policyholder avail of tax benefits under this plan?
As per extant tax laws, this plan offers tax benefits under Section 80C, 80D (for specific rider premium) and Section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961. Please note that all the tax benefits are subject to tax laws prevailing at the time of payment of premium or receipt of benefits by

The following details are about Akshaya Plan(AP).
 Please note that all the tax benefits are subject to tax laws prevailing at the time of payment of premium or receipt of benefits by policyholder. It is advisable to seek an independent tax advice.
Premium Discontinuance
41. What happens if policyholder does not pay premiums on time?
If policyholder is unable to pay premium by the due date, he / she will be given a grace period of 30 days (15 days for monthly mode policies) and during this grace period all coverage under this policy will continue.
42. What happens if policyholder does not pay premium within the grace period?
If policyholder does not pay premium within the grace period, the following will be applicable:
(a) In case policyholder has not paid premiums for two full policy years then policy will lapse and all benefits under this policy will cease immediately.
(b) In case policyholder has paid premiums for at least two full policy years then policy will continue on a Reduced Paid-Up basis.
43. What is a Reduced Paid-up policy?
If policyholder discontinues paying premiums, after having paid premiums for at least two full years, policy will not lapse but

The following details are about Akshaya Plan(AP).

If policyholder discontinues paying premiums, after having paid premiums for at least two full years, policy will not lapse but will continue on a Reduced Paid-Up basis. After the policy has entered RPU mode, the benefits payable will be amended as follows:
The RPU Sum Assured shall be equal to the Sum Assured multiplied by the RPU Factor,
where the RPU Factor is the ratio of:
- The number of premium instalments paid to date; over
- The total number of premium instalments originally payable during the Policy Term
44. What would be the death benefit for a Reduced Paid-up policy during the policy term? If the Life Insured dies during the Policy Term, the RPU Death Benefit will be as follows:
The nominee(s)/beneficiary will be paid:
• The RPU Sum Assured on Death plus
• Accumulated Cash Bonus (if declared)
Upon payment of the above RPU Death Benefit, the policy will terminate. The “RPU Sum Assured on Death” will be the Sum Assured on Death multiplied by the RPU factor, subject to minimum 105% of Total Premiums Paid up to the date of death.

The following details are about Akshaya Plan(AP).
 The “RPU Sum Assured on Death” will be the Sum Assured on Death multiplied by the RPU factor, subject to minimum 105% of Total Premiums Paid up to the date of death.
45. What would be the Maturity benefit for a reduced paid-up policy? Maturity Benefit for RPU Policies:
In the event the Life Insured survives to the end of the Policy Term, the following shall be payable:
• The RPU Sum Assured on Death plus
• Accumulated Cash Bonus (if declared)
Note: Reduced Paid-Up policies will not participate in future bonuses. Once the policy becomes RPU, all rider benefits will cease.

Policyholder has a period of 15 days (30 days in case of electronic policies and the policies issued under the provisions of IRDAI Guidelines on Distance Marketing of Insurance products) from the date of receipt of the policy document, to review the terms and conditions of the policy.

policies issued under the provisions of IRDAI Guidelines on Distance Marketing of Insurance products) from the date of receipt of the policy document, to review the terms and conditions of the policy. Termination

• the date of payment of the Surrender Value; or
• the date of settlement of the Death Benefit; or
• end of the Policy Term; or
• the date on which the Revival period ends after Your policy has lapsed as per Premium Discontinuance
provision; or

Operational Guidelines
48. Is there any additional benefit in this product for ABG employees?
Yes, for employees of Aditya Birla Group companies, agents of Aditya Birla Sun Life Insurance and employees of Corporate Agents and Brokers, an enhancement of 3% on the Cash Bonus, is payable if they buy their policies directly from ABSLI. No commission is payable on these policies.
Example: The annual cash bonus payable to a 45-year-old policyholder buying a Whole Life Income policy with PPT 10 years, maturity age 100 years and Annualized Premium of Rs. 1,00,000 is Rs. 34,980. If the policyholder, is an employee of ABSLI, the annual cash bonus payable shall be 1.03*34,980 = Rs. 36,029.
49. If ABG employee also has an existing policy with ABSLI, then which discount will be applicable? Employee, who is also an existing customer of ABSLI, will get both discounts.
50.

The following details are about Akshaya Plan(AP).
 If ABG employee also has an existing policy with ABSLI, then which discount will be applicable? Employee, who is also an existing customer of ABSLI, will get both discounts.
50. What application form is required for ABSLI Assured Income Plus?
The “Common Application Form - MAJOR” and “Common Application Form - MINOR” would be used for this plan.
51. Can Life Insured and Policyholder be different under this plan?
Yes, the Life Insured and Policyholder can be different under this plan. In case Policyholder and the Life Insured are different then all the proceeds for the policy will be paid to the Policyholder.
52. Does this plan also include auto-vesting functionality for minor lives?
Yes, where a policy is issued on a minor life, the policy will vest after attainment of majority (age 18 years) of the Life Insured.
53. How is this plan different from the ABSLI VisionLife Income Plus Plan?
Under ‘ABSLI Vision LifeIncome Plus Plan’ we declare bonuses in the form of Paid Up Additions every year & the customer can opt to take the Cash Value of Paid Up Additions. However, under ‘ABSLI Akshaya Plan’, the default option is Cash Bonus which can be availed at the end

The following details are about Akshaya Plan(AP).
 However, under ‘ABSLI Akshaya Plan’, the default option is Cash Bonus which can be availed at the end of the year or as per the cash bonus payout frequency chosen.

‘ABSLI Vision LifeIncome Plus Plan’ also has a Short Term Income option which is not available under ‘ABSLI Akshaya Plan’. Also, under ‘ABSLI Akshaya Plan’ we are introducing a short pay option i.e. 6 pay, wherein customers can pay for 6 years and can get coverage for up to 100 years, which is not available under ‘ABSLI Vision LifeIncome Plus Plan’.
54. What will be the target customer segment for this product?
a) The product caters to the need of customers with ‘Low to Moderate’ risk-profile.
b) This product aims to cater to the immediate liquidity needs of the customer by providing Cash Bonus from the end of first year and the bonus pay-outs can be customized basis one’s life-stage requirements.

The following details are about Akshaya Plan(AP).

b) This product aims to cater to the immediate liquidity needs of the customer by providing Cash Bonus from the end of first year and the bonus pay-outs can be customized basis one’s life-stage requirements.
c) The minimum ticket size for the product is Rs. 50,000 and it aims to target:-
• Persons looking for a steady source of second income
• Businessmen looking for instant liquidity, ensuring a comfortable lifestyle for their family
• Parents looking to plan for their child’s future
55. What is the medical grid applicable for this plan? Medical grid of investment plans is applicable for this plan.

FNIO allowed (however client has to be in India)

As is the case for all savings plan, the GST Rate for first year premiums will be 4.5% p.a. and from second policy year onwards, it will be 2.25% p.a.

59. Does this plan cover death due to COVID19? Yes, provided there is no non-disclosure on the COVID Q. Please ensure that the COVID questionnaire is filled.
60. Does this plan cover death which happened in a war?
Yes, the policy covers death under all situations (including death during declared or undeclared war, civil commotion, invasion, terrorism, hostilities) except death due to suicide as specified in the suicide clause.
